title: The Problem With Payment Solutions for Iranian Digital Sellers and How to Solve It
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: Iranian digital sellers face a significant challenge: traditional payment solutions like PayPal and Stripe have blocked their access due to US sanctions, leaving them unable to receive payments from international customers. This not only hinders their ability to monetize their digital products but also limits their global reach and impact. Many have turned to alternative payment methods, only to find them cumbersome, unreliable, and often involving unnecessary intermediaries. The result is a loss of potential revenue, a reduced online presence, and an inability to tap into the vast international market for digital goods.
The Real Reason This Problem Exists

The problem lies not with the digital sellers themselves, but with the outdated payment infrastructure that has failed to keep pace with the evolving global landscape. This infrastructure was designed with a different era in mind, one where geographical and financial boundaries were more rigid. However, with the rise of digital technologies, these boundaries have become increasingly blurred, leaving traditional payment solutions struggling to adapt.
Why the Obvious Fixes Do Not Work

The P2P exchange ecosystem in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, and Pakistan has matured significantly alongside crypto adoption. Platforms like Binance P2P, local exchanges, and mobile money integrations allow creators to convert USDT, BTC, and other coins to naira, cedis, Kenyan shillings, and Pakistani rupees at near-market rates. Transactions typically complete within 30 minutes. The conversion fees are significantly lower than what PayPal or Western Union charge on the same corridor. This means the practical outcome of receiving crypto is not that you are holding volatile assets — it is that you are getting paid in a form you can immediately convert to local currency at a competitive rate.
The Infrastructure That Solves It

GitHub Pages provides free, reliable hosting for static sites with no server management required. A digital store built on GitHub Pages with the Core Shop Engine has no monthly hosting cost, scales automatically to any traffic level, and can be deployed from anywhere in the world. The combination of free hosting, crypto payments through NOWPayments, and automated delivery through the Core Shop Engine creates a store infrastructure with a total cost structure that is fundamentally different from platform-based alternatives. There are no percentage fees on sales, no monthly subscription costs, and no platform that can decide to delist your products based on content policy changes.
Traditional payment solutions have failed to keep pace with the evolving global landscape.
How to Implement the Solution Today

To implement this solution, Iranian digital sellers can start by integrating the Web3 Wallet Connect frontend into their website or online store. This involves a simple and user-friendly setup process that can be completed in just a few minutes. Next, they can set up a NOWPayments account, which will provide them with a unique API key that can be used to receive payments in various cryptocurrencies. Finally, they can link their NOWPayments account to their online store, ensuring a seamless payment experience for their customers.
What Success Looks Like After the Fix

With the blockchain-based payment infrastructure in place, Iranian digital sellers can finally tap into the vast international market for digital goods. They can receive payments from customers worldwide, without the need for intermediaries or complex verification processes. This not only opens up new revenue streams but also enables them to build a stronger online presence, expand their customer base, and increase their global impact.
How can creators in Africa sell digital products internationally?

The most practical path for creators in restricted countries is a combination of NOWPayments for payment processing and USDT on Tron for the primary payment coin. USDT is a stable coin pegged to the US dollar, which means your revenue does not fluctuate with crypto market movements. Tron network fees are under one dollar, and settlement takes under 60 seconds. Once you receive USDT in your wallet, you convert to local currency through P2P exchanges — Binance P2P, Paxful, or local platforms specific to your country. The conversion typically takes 20-30 minutes and costs 2-5% of the transaction, which is significantly less than PayPal's international transfer fees or Western Union's rates. This is the complete payment flow that thousands of creators in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, and Bangladesh are using right now.
Blockchain-based payment infrastructure offers a seamless and scalable solution for international transactions.

Iranian digital sellers can finally tap into the vast international market for digital goods with the right infrastructure in place.
